1.description: Required CBDC efficiency advantage in baseline. This figure plots histograms ofα★$\alpha ^\star$(dark gray) andα$\alpha$(light gray); 2,000,000 draws each, 1,000 bins, logarithmic scale.

2.description: Required CBDC efficiency advantage with pass-through costs. This figure plots histograms ofα★p$\alpha ^{\star \mathrm{p}}$(dark gray) andαp$\alpha ^\mathrm{p}$(light gray) wheno=10ρ$o = 10 \rho$; 2,000,000 draws each, 1,000 bins, logarithmic scale.

3.description: Required CBDC efficiency advantage with pass-through costs and tax distortions. This figure plots histograms ofα★pt$\alpha ^{\star \mathrm{pt}}$(dark gray) andαpt$\alpha ^\mathrm{pt}$(light gray) wheno=10ρ$o = 10 \rho$andx=0.25$x = 0.25$; 2,000,000 draws each, 1,000 bins, logarithmic scale.

4.description: Optimal portfolio shares. This figure produces a contour plot of the median ofm★/n★$m^\star / n^\star$in(o/ρ,η)$(o / \rho, \eta)$space whenμ=ν+ρ$\mu = \nu + \rho$; 2,000,000 draws. Numbers in boxes indicate contour levels.

5.description: Liquidity substitution costs. This figure showsω(ζ,ζ)$\omega (\zeta, \zeta)$for 500 calibration draws. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Demand for reserves. This figure showsχr(ζ)$\chi ^r(\zeta)$for 500 calibration draws. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Reserve operating costs. This figure plots a histogram ofρ$\rho$realizations; the calibration is based on Fedwire costs (light gray) and central bank cost shares (dark gray); 1,000,000 draws each, 1,000 bins, logarithmic scale.
